SME, acquired by the Safran Group last year, specializes in the area of energetic material while Snecma is a specialist in solid rocket motors. texts.full_text: sphincter (EUS) for treating detrusor external sphincter dyssynergia (DESD) in men with spinal cord injury (SCI). Descriptive study. Hospital rehabilitation department. Patients (N=15; mean age, 40.5y; range, 22-64y) with suprasacral SCI with confirmed DESD determined by urodynamic study. A single dose of 100U BTX-A was injected into theEUS using an ultrasound-guided trocar catheter transurethral approach under local anesthesia and sedation if necessary. Methods: Participants underwent detailed baseline assessments, including neurological examination, bladder diary analysis, quality-of-life questionnaires (e.g., SF-36), urodynamic studies to confirm DESD presence. The procedure involved sterile preparation of the urethra followed by insertion of a trocar catheter guided under transrectal ultrasound imaging until it reached the level where external sphincter was visualized clearly. After identifying appropriate EUS regions, BTX-A diluted in saline solution (total volume 10ml; final concentration approximately 5U/0.1 ml) containing one unit per microliter of fluid, were injected into both sides and midline portions if required or where dyssynergia was most prominent as determined by pre-procedure urodynamic data. Post-injection follow-up included weekly patient diaries for micturition events over 3 months alongside regular clinical check-ups including residual urine measurement after voiding to evaluate treatment impact. Participants also completed post-treatment quality-of-life and symptom-specific surveys at month three compared with baseline measures prior injection day findings regarding frequency urgency incontinence as well overall lower urinary tract functional status change. Results: Preliminary data indicated significant reduction in peak urethral pressure recorded during detrusion indicating decreased sphincter contraction. Additionally, decrease incidence of urologic complications like recurrent UTIs or stones was noted amongst cohort members suggesting improvement bladder emptying efficiency without exacerbating problems stemming from poor residual volume clearance between micturitions. Conclusion: Transrectal ultrasound-guided BTX-A injection via trocar catheter approach appears promising for treating DESD symptoms in men with SCI offering an interventional alternative potentially enhancing quality-of-life through alleviating dysuria and urinary retention risk factors associated complex voiding dynamics following spinal cord damage. But she was instrumental in setting the tone for all of our work. Ida was a great friend. She never stopped talking about Greenwood...and mesmerized us during many late night story and song circles. A raw, hilarious, brilliant and organic organizer; she was driven by a powerful sense of justice. She galvanized us all...and gave us the courage we needed to do what had to be done. Her spirit will always be with me. Heather Baum St. Paul, Minnesota Endesha Ida Mae Holland Dies Greenwood Commonwealth 2006 Playwright and teacher Endesha Ida Mae Holland will come back to Greenwood one last time. "I don't think her heart ever really left Greenwood That was home," said her friend and manager Habibi Minnie Wilson. Holland, an award-winning dramatist whose play "From the Mississippi Delta" was nominated for a Pulitzer Prize, died in her sleep Wednesday, Jan. 25, 2006, in a nursing facility in Los Angeles. She was 61. There are several ways in which the word feels strange for me to hear or use. I often get asked, are you proud of your work, whether it’s “Anyone But Me” or anything else I’ve done. Sometimes Sometimes feeling pride can be a double-edged sword—while on one hand experiencing Pride in accomplishments and self is crucial for personal growth; yet excessive boasts of the same might come across as arrogance to others around you, making it hard balance at times. This isn't only peculiar but also requires patience understanding from both sides involved

WO2004/103299 discloses a method of improving functional recovery following a central nervous system contusion injury. The disclosed invention is directed to a method of utilizing chondroitinase (chondroitin sulfate degrading enzyme) to promote autonomic neurological functional recovery following injury in or to the spinal cord. Compositions useful in the method include acceptable formulations of chondroitinase. The method includes administering a therapeutically effective amount of glycosaminoglycan degrading enzyme. The glycosaminoglycan degrading enzyme may be dermatan sulfate or chondroitin sulfate degrading enzymes. The functional recovery may include autonomic functions, sensory functions, motor functions or the like. WO2005/087920 relates to recombinant and modified chondroitinase ABC I, their production and their uses. The disclosed chondroitinase ABC I enzymes are useful for a variety of purposes, including therapeutic methods such as promoting nerve regeneration, promoting stroke recovery, treating spinal cord injury, treating epithelial disease, treating infections and treating cancer. Other approaches to CSPG inhibition have focused on the use of molecules/agents that inhibit the interaction of CSPGs with its receptor RPTPσ. WO2011/022462 discloses the use of soluble fragments of RPTPs that bind CSPGs, thus acting as competitive inhibitors to prevent the CSPGs from binding RPTPs on the neuron. The neural cell can be associated with an injury or neurodegenerative condition. WO2012/112953 discloses methods for contacting a neuron with an agent that binds RPTPσ, to thereby induce neuronal outgrowth of the neuron. The agent may induce clustering of RPTPσ and/or inhibit binding of CSPGs to RPTPσ. Examples of suitable agents are heparan sulfate proteoglycan, heparan sulfate, heparan sulfate oligosaccharides, or heparin oligosaccharides. For nervous system injuries there are substantial patient populations with significant unmet needs, for which novel treatment options are desperately required. There is currently no treatment for recovering human nerve function after injury to the central nervous system. Secondary injury mechanisms have, so far, been predominantly targeted through the use of neuroprotective treatments. texts.full_text: during Martial Law in the Philippines, influenced Benito de Leon to join the Philippine Military Academy himself. Education and Early Career De León graduated from PMA as a member of the "Makatao" Class of 1983, alongside fellow peace advocate Maj. Gen. Carlito Galvez Jr., who would later become the Chief of Staff of the Armed Forces of the Philippines. After graduating, De León was assigned to various military posts in Mindanao, where he gained extensive experience in counterinsurgency operations against communist and Muslim separatist rebels. Peace Efforts and Honors De León's commitment to peace and development led him to engage with local communities, particularly the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) and other Moro groups. His efforts in promoting dialogue and understanding between the military and these groups earned him the trust of both sides. In recognition of his contributions, he was conferred the title "Sultan a Romapunut" by the Maranaos, an honorary title reserved for those who have significantly contributed to peace and unity among their people. In 2013, De León played a crucial role in the implementation of the Comprehensive Agreement on the Bangsamoro (CAB), a significant step towards addressing the long-standing conflict in Mindanao. His leadership in this process further solidified his reputation as a key figure in the pursuit of peace and reconciliation in the Philippines. Retirement and Post-Military Life After retiring from military service, De León continued to be involved in various initiatives aimed at promoting peace and development in Mindanao. He has been actively engaged with several non-government organizations (NGOs) and private sector entities that work towards the same goals. His extensive network and experience have made him a valuable asset in these endeavors, furthering his commitment to bringing sustainable change to conflict-affected areas. Legacy Benito de Leon's career is marked by his dedication to fostering peace through dialogue and cooperation. His unique approach has not only earned him respect from both military and civilia

A large amount of displacement of the multi-layer piezoelectric element as a whole is achieved as all piezoelectric material layers act while maintaining firm contact with each other with internal electrodes interposed therebetween. texts.full_text: p stamp shows HMS Jaguar. In early 1962, The Royal Society mounted an expedition to Tristan to study the effects of the volcano on the flora and fauna of the island, as well as to study the volcanology of the island. The expedition had no remit to consider the possibility of the islanders returning. Prior to the expedition HMS Jaguar visited the island on 16th December 1961 to make a preliminary investigation before the main expedition departed. The 95p stamp shows MV Tristania. The MV Tristania was heavily involved during the entire volcano period. One of two fishing vessels licensed to fish Tristan waters, she was off-shore at the time of the eruption on 10th October 1961, and was instrumental in evacuating the islanders to nearby Nightingale Island to await further evacuation to safety. On 8th September 1962, she landed the Resettlement Survey team on Tristan and on 9th April 1963, she assisted in the return of the first resettlement party of 51 islanders. The £1.10p stamp shows the MV Stirling Castle. Once safely evacuated from Tristan, the islanders were taken to Cape Town, where they embarked on the MV Stirling Castle and departed for England on 21st October 1961. texts.full_text: allies. After signing the nonaggression pact with Germany and defeating the Kwangtung Army at Khalkin Gol, Russia started to withdraw its help from China. Soon relations were made with the USA who only slowly began to (officially) provide help and even more when America entered the war. More than 600 of the T16 CTMS tanks were supposed to be delivered to China under Lend-Lease after Pearl Harbor, the logistical difficulty and its obsolete design compelled the Chinese to reject the offer. Later 233 U.S. M2A4 light tanks were acquired by the Nationalists along with some 48 M3A3, M5A1 Stuart tanks in Lend/Lease from the USA in 1943, and 35 M4A4 Sherman tanks were acquired under the United States Military Assistance Program between 1943 and 1944. Upon the American entry into the war in 1941, it began to supply China with AFVs which the Soviets were unable to provide. M3 Stuarts and M4 Shermans trickled in through Burma and formed part of the several well-equipped, well-trained armies that the Nationalists could deploy. These units were responsible for stopping numerous Japanese attacks during the later phases of the war. The 200th Division also saw action in the Burma Theatre under Joseph Stilwell; it participated in the Battle of Yunnan-Burma Road in 1942 in the Burma campaign. History Pro Patria was founded in 1909 as the Verein Schweizerische Bundesfeier-Spende (Swiss Federal Celebration Donation Association) by a group of public-spirited citizens led by the merchant Albert Schuster. The association's efforts were supported by the Swiss federal government and the Post Office, and Pro Patria coordinated its activities with the government during the first decades of its operation. The first Federal Celebration Collection yielded 29,000 Swiss francs and was used to support flood victims. In the patriotic atmosphere brought about by the outbreak of World War I, Pro Patria'''s collections gained the support of other associations and their volunteers, including the Swiss Samaritans (Schweizerisches Hilfswerk) and the Schweizerische Gemeinnützige Gesellschaft. Translate to English English: Taguchi and Ishii conducted experiments on the respiration, excretion, and grazing behavior in two copepod species - Calanus cristatus (Copepoda) and C. plumchrus – within Northern North Pacific waters during a period of time known as '70s. In their research paper published by Japan Society for Fisheries Science from Tokyo under the title "The Biology Of Marine Plankton Organisms: Researches Carried Out By Japanese Scientists During The ’70's" they focused on studying three key aspects related to these two copepod species: 1. Respiration Respiratory rates were measured in both Calanus cristatus and C. plumchrus during the course of their experiments at sea, aboard a research vessel. The respiration rate represents how much oxygen is consumed by an organism over time. 2.Excretion: Ishii & Taguchi also studied excretory behavior - that's to say they looked into waste production in these two copepod species. The process was observed throughout the experiment period and analyzed for any possible patterns or trends. Excretion refers broadly, but specifically here it means expulsion of metabolic wastes from organisms. 3.Grazing: Another key focus area among their experiments on both Calanus cristatus (Copepoda) and C.pumchrus in Northern North Pacific was grazing behavior. Grazers are those living beings which directly consume plants or algae. texts.full_text: 002, and May 1, 2015, were reviewed. Age, sex, visual symptoms, number and types of surgical treatments, cavernous sinus involvement, complications, duration of follow-up, residual tumor, use of adjuvant radiation therapy, and extent of proptosis resolution as measured by the exophthalmos index (EI) pre- and postoperatively and at the final follow-up were recorded. RESULTS Thirty-three patients (24 female [73%]) with an average age of 51.6 years were treated for MAP. Of the 22 patients with additional visual symptoms (for example, loss of visual acuity, field cut, or diplopia), 15 had improved vision and 7 had stable vision. No patients had worse proptosis after treatment. The average preoperative EI was 1.39, the average immediate postoperative EI was 1.23, and the average final EI at the most recent follow-up was 1.13. Thus, average overall EI improvement was 0.26, but the average immediate EI reduction was 0.16, demonstrating that proptosis progressively improved during the postoperative period. Residual cavernous sinus involvement was present in 17 of 18 patients who had had preoperative cavernous sinus meningioma involvement. Only 2 patients in the series had recurrent tumor at the orbital region, and their proptosis improved again after reoperation. One case of delayed vasospasm and 2 cases of postoperative trigeminal numbness (V2) were recorded. The average follow-up was 4.5 years (53.8 months). CONCLUSIONS In this series, all patients experienced proptosis improvement and none had worse visual symptoms at the final follow-up, although proptosis resolution occurred over time. I would like to identify work flow deviations from call graphs generated during profiliDynamic analysis is an essential aspect when it comes Web Application security testing as its focus lies in the runtime behavior, which can reveal crucial insights about potential vulnerabilities within a live application's execution path and workflow logic that might be difficult or impossible with static methods alone. Here are some tools you could consider for dynamic web-application assessments: 1) ZAP (Zed Attack Proxy): An open source tool from OWASP provides on the fly scanning of applications as they run, enabling identification not just vulnerabilities but also anomalies in workflows due its ability intercept requests/responses during active sessions. 2.Burp Suite: This popular suite offers an Intruder feature specifically designed for attacking parameters within web apps through automated custom attack scenarios which can help uncover unexpected behaviors or deviations from normal flow patterns when certain conditions are met dynamically 3)Dynatrace: While primarily known as APM solution, Dynatrace provides detailed transaction tracking capabilities that allow analysts monitor every step taken by end-users inside complex enterprise grade PHP applications helping spot any aberrations easily. 4.) texts.full_text: o immunity for its failure to erect warning signs or otherwise warn drivers of the dangerous nature of an intersection. Id. at 50-51. But the supreme court has since limited this holding to situations where a municipality's "failure to act * * * is based on the municipality's failure to maintain its streets in a reasonably safe condition." Nusbaum, 422 N.W.2d at 76 (citing Larson, 290 N.W.2d at 50-51). Here, appellant's claims are not based on the county's failure to maintain its streets in a reasonably safe condition. Therefore, the trial court properly concluded that the county was immune from liability for any tort claim arising out of its failure to erect warning signs or otherwise warn drivers of the dangerous nature of the intersection. Appellant also argues that the county is not entitled to immunity because it had a duty to install a traffic control device at the intersection. Appellant relies on Larson v. Independent Sch. Dist. No. 314, Braham, 290 N.W.2d 46 (Minn.1980). In Larson, the supreme court held that a municipality is not entitled to immunity for its failure to install a traffic control device at an intersection. texts.full_text: Zubeida Mustafa THE ONE factor which has proved to be a major impediment to the socioeconomic development of countries in the Third World is population explosion. It has now been conclusively established that Governments which have been incapable or unwilling to slow down the population growth rate in their countries have failed to bring about much of a change in the quality of life of their people — whatever be the growth recorded in the sectors which are taken to constitute the indices of economic progress. Conversely, failure to promote the socio-economic development of people results in a high population growth rate. This was the message to emerge from the seminar on women, children and population held in early November at Jakarta, cosponsored by UNICEF, U’NFPA, the Press Foundation of Asia and the Indonesian Planned Parenthood Association. The seminar in which ... participants representing 21 Asian countries took part was designed to focus attention on a crucial issue — population planning as an essential element for all-round betterment of people's lives. The conference underscored that effective family planning is not merely about controlling birth rates but integrally linked with enhancing the wellbeing and potential of women, children, and communities at large. texts.full_text: central portion of the text: "You Never Think It Will Happen to You": Health Workers Confront the Risks of Tuberculosis Patricia Bond was working as a nurse at a health facility in South Africa when she developed some troubling symptoms. She began coughing up blood and experiencing severe fatigue, leading her to seek medical attention. After undergoing several tests, Patricia received the devastating diagnosis of multidrug-resistant tuberculosis (MDR-TB). The disease and treatment robbed her of her hearing and the occupation she loved—she’s no longer able to provide care for patients. Despite the challenges she faced, Patricia remained determined to raise awareness about TB and its impact on health workers like herself. She shared her story with others, highlighting the need for better protection and support for those at risk of exposure. In South Africa, where TB is rampant and often linked to HIV/AIDS, Patricia's experience is all too common. Health workers are particularly vulnerable due to their close contact with patients who may be infected. The disease can have a profound impact on those diagnosed, as well as their loved ones and the broader community. And just by going to work every day, health workers like Patricia face major risk of exposure. Today, Patricia is lucky to be alive. The

texts.full_text: Dam is not only an engineering marvel but a symbol of China's rapid industrialization and modernity since the late 20th century. It represents both immense pride for its builders—a testament to human ingenuity and technological advancement—and significant controversy due to displacement issues and environmental concerns, such as changes in river flow that have affected fish migration patterns. While it is celebrated by many for providing a massive source of renewable energy and improving flood control along the Yangtze River basin, critics point out its impact on local communities who lost their homes and livelihoods. The relocation process has been fraught with challenges, including inadequate compensation packages and difficulties in finding suitable new housing. Despite these complexities, there is no denying that TGD's sheer scale makes it a remarkable achievement in the field of hydroelectric engineering. It stands as an emblematic structure not just for China but globally, where its lessons on sustainability, environmental impact assessment, community engagement, and disaster management are studied by engineers worldwide. The dam has also prompted discussions about energy consumption patterns across nations; with such vast amounts of electricity at their disposal, the question becomes how to use this power sustainably—ensuring it contributes positively towards a low-carbon future while mitigating any negative environmental impacts. As global climate change continues to pose challenges for all countries, TGD's role in China’s energy strategy will remain under scrutiny. This blog serves as a platform for students in the Architecture and Interior Architecture programs to share their experiences, projects, and insights throughout their first year of studies. Today, we are featuring a visit to Stewart Milne Timber Systems, a leading timber manufacturing company, which provided our students with a unique opportunity to learn about building materials and innovative design solutions. During the visit, students had the chance to explore the various stages of timber production, from raw materials to finished products. They were able to witness the precision and craftsmanship involved in creating high-quality timber components, which are used in a range of architectural projects. The visit also included a presentation by Stewart Milne Timber Systems' team, who discussed the company's commitment to sustainability and its role in promoting the use of timber as a renewable resource in the construction industry. This experience was invaluable for our students, as it allowed them to see the practical applications of the theories and principles they are in the classroom. They were able to gain a deeper understanding of the importance of sustainable design and the role of timber in creating environmentally buildings. We would like to extend our sincere thanks to Stewart Milne Timber Systems for hosting this visit and for providing our students with such a rich and learning experience. By supplying liquid fuel and gaseous fuel to an engine when a pressure of a tank is less than a threshold, it may be possible to draw additional gas from the storage tank while providing liquid fuel to the engine so that the engine does not misfire or operate richer than is desired. For example, gas may flow from the storage tank to the engine at a lower rate than is desirable for operating the engine solely with the gaseous fuel.
